title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power development board with an integrated Coral NPU accelerator designed for on-device AI inference. It ships with a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, and supports running Gemma models for applications like live translation, natural language control, and multimodal AI workloads entirely on the board.
writeup: The video demonstrates running Gemma models on Google's Coral Board, a low-power development board with an integrated Coral NPU accelerator built for on-device AI inference. The hardware ships with a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, supporting multimodal workloads that run entirely on the board. Demonstrated applications include live translation and natural language control. The takeaway: Gemma inference can execute locally on dedicated edge hardware without offloading to the cloud.
